name: trimmy
title: Trimmy
description: "Paste once, run once." — Trimmy flattens those multi-line shell snippets you copy so they actually paste and run.
author: chrismessina

commands.name: trim
commands.title: Trim and Copy
commands.subtitle: Trimmy
commands.description: Clean clipboard text by flattening multi-line commands into a runnable one-liner.

preferences.name: aggressiveness
preferences.title: Aggressiveness
preferences.description: Controls how eagerly Trim cleans text that looks like a command.

preferences.name: preferSelectionFallback
preferences.title: Prefer Selection / Finder
preferences.description: When enabled, Trim uses selected text first, then a selected Finder item path, then the clipboard.
preferences.label: Prefer selected text or Finder item before clipboard

commands.name: trim-and-paste
commands.title: Trim and Paste
commands.subtitle: Trimmy
commands.description: Clean text and immediately paste the result into the frontmost app.

preferences.name: aggressiveness
preferences.title: Aggressiveness
preferences.description: Controls how eagerly Trim cleans text that looks like a command.

preferences.name: preferSelectionFallback
preferences.title: Prefer Selection / Finder
preferences.description: When enabled, Trim uses selected text first, then a selected Finder item path, then the clipboard.
preferences.label: Prefer selected text or Finder item before clipboard

commands.name: preview-trim
commands.title: Trim and Preview
commands.subtitle: Trimmy
commands.description: Preview the trimmed result before copying or pasting it.

preferences.name: aggressiveness
preferences.title: Aggressiveness
preferences.description: Controls how eagerly Trim cleans text that looks like a command.

preferences.name: preferSelectionFallback
preferences.title: Prefer Selection / Finder
preferences.description: When enabled, Trim uses selected text first, then a selected Finder item path, then the clipboard.
preferences.label: Prefer selected text or Finder item before clipboard

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
